title: split_var doesn't seem to work
body:
I know that DATA_WIDTH > 1 and LENGTH > 0, so neither dimension of this declaration is width-1. But including `split_var` results in the following error:

```
%Warning-SPLITVAR: /some/path/some_file.v:86:25: 'axis_tdata' has split_var metacomment but will not be split because its bitwidth is 1. : ... note: In instance 'axis_pipeline_register'
   86 |   wire [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] axis_tdata [0:LENGTH]  /*verilator split_var*/ ;
      |                         ^~~~~~~~~~
                   ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/SPLITVAR?v=5.042
                   ... Use "/* verilator lint_off SPLITVAR */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```
code_blocks:
%Warning-SPLITVAR: /some/path/some_file.v:86:25: 'axis_tdata' has split_var metacomment but will not be split because its bitwidth is 1. : ... note: In instance 'axis_pipeline_register'
   86 |   wire [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] axis_tdata [0:LENGTH]  /*verilator split_var*/ ;
      |                         ^~~~~~~~~~
                   ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/SPLITVAR?v=5.042
                   ... Use "/* verilator lint_off SPLITVAR */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
